notes: NOAA CPC operational bands. ≤0.5 neutral; 0.5–1.0 weak El Niño (watch); 1.0–1.5 moderate (alert); >1.5 strong/very strong (emergency-class). Symmetric La Niña handled by absolute value upstream.
NOAA CPC standardized SOI (range ~-4..+4, NOT BoM's ×10 scale). Sustained negative = El Niño. >-0.7 neutral; -0.7 to -1.0 watch; -1.0 to -1.5 alert; <-1.5 strong event. NOAA -1.0 ≈ BoM -10.

unit: m/s 850 mb zonal wind anomaly (West Pacific)
notes: NOAA CPC wpac850. Negative = weakened/westerly trades = warm water pushed east = leading El Niño trigger (precedes SST by weeks–months). >-0.5 neutral; -0.5 to -1.0 watch; -1.0 to -1.5 alert; <-1.5 strong westerly burst regime.

LIVE — NMME dynamical forecast (NOAA-GFDL SPEAR ensemble mean, via IRI CCSR open OPeNDAP). Projected Niño-3.4 SST anomaly for the forward 3-month window. Same bands as observed ONI; symmetric (El Niño high / La Niña low). Forward-looking complement to the observed ONI gauge. As a forecast it never raises the CURRENT national alert (see risk-engine NON_ALERT_KEYS).
Niño 3.4 monthly SST anomaly. Aligned with ONI; included separately so weekly SST can escalate before the 3-month ONI does.
PNG highland drought thresholds. -20% near-normal; -40% drought watch; -60% severe drought (1997 and 2015 events both >-60%). Inverted: more negative = worse.
Heat-stress proxy. Combined with rainfall deficit it drives crop and health risk in the highlands.
NASA SMAP-derived percentile. Inverted: lower percentile = drier = worse. <10th percentile = exceptional drought.
MODIS NDVI anomaly. Vegetation stress lags rainfall by ~4–8 weeks; an early canary for food-security collapse.

unit: count of M4.5+ events in PNG (30-day window)
notes: USGS FDSNWS earthquake tempo for the PNG bbox. PNG sits on the Ring of Fire; a typical month sees ~10–25 M4.5+ events. Elevated tempo (>25) flags a seismically active period; a major shock or swarm pushes >45. Non-inverted, one-sided: higher count = worse, never negative.
